---
title: "NGordnet"
subtitle: "Google Books Ngram + Princeton WordNet at scale"
date: "2025-06-01"
categories: [backend, data-engineering, full-stack]
description: "Spring Boot + PostgreSQL service processing 176M+ rows of Google Books Ngram V3 data against Princeton WordNet."
image: /assets/projects/ngordnet-thumb.png
---

[Demo](https://ngordnet.example.com) · [GitHub](https://github.com/SamarthSheth/ngordnet)

A Spring Boot + PostgreSQL service that joins the **Google Books Ngram V3 corpus** (176M+ rows) with **Princeton WordNet** to answer queries like *"plot the historical frequency of all hyponyms of 'transport'"*.

## What it does

- Ingests Google Ngram V3 (split across files by leading character) and WordNet's noun hierarchy into Postgres.
- Indexes by `(word, year)` and walks the WordNet DAG to expand a query word to its hyponym set.
- Returns time-series frequency data ready for plotting.

## Design notes

- **Bulk loading.** Streamed `COPY FROM` instead of `INSERT`, dropped indexes during ingest, rebuilt after. About a 40× speedup vs naive.
- **Hyponym expansion.** WordNet's IS-A relation is a DAG, not a tree. Cycles don't exist but diamonds do — naive recursion double-counts. Used a recursive CTE with a visited set.
- **Caching.** Hyponym expansion is pure; cached at the application layer keyed on the synset.

## Stack

Spring Boot · PostgreSQL · Flyway migrations · Docker · Railway.

## Repository

[github.com/SamarthSheth/ngordnet](https://github.com/SamarthSheth/ngordnet)
